summary: High‑energy show with blistering Let It Grow, double‑time C.C. Rider
blurb: The set bursts open with a driving Alabama Getaway into a scorching Let It Grow and a double‑time C.C. Rider that sets the night on fire. Highlights include seamless segues through Shakedown Street > Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance, a soaring Big Railroad Blues, and an expansive Wheel/Playing In The Band sandwich. Jerry’s incendiary leads and Brent’s bright keyboards keep the momentum relentless.
review: December 5‑1981 at Market Square Arena captured one of the Dead’s most ferocious nights of the era. From the opening “Alabama Getaway” into a tight “C.C. Rider” played in double‑time, the band never let up – Jerry Garcia’s guitar was on fire, laying down scorching leads on “Let It Grow”, “Shakedown Street” and the extended “Playing In The Band” sandwiches. Phil Lesh’s bass thundered, especially on “Passenger” and the big “Cumberland Blues” groove, while Brent Mydland added his signature bright, twinkling keys that lifted the jams on “Big Railroad Blues” and the “Wheel”. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann forged a thundering drum/space wall that propelled the second‑set flow, making the segues feel almost seamless. The night’s high points – the double‑time C.C. Rider, the Bird Song → Let It Grow surge, the Shakedown > Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance sequence and the soaring “Wheel” sandwich – are captured with vivid detail in the best soundboard source, making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead seeking that 1981 high‑octane magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All ten recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising the same songs (Let It Grow, C.C. Rider double‑time, Shakedown Street, Bird Song, The Wheel) and band dynamics. Only one recording is mixed; no consistent negative performance notes appear. This consensus yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture described as clear, crisp and ‘exquisite’, preserving the band’s high energy without drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Double‑time C.C. Rider that launches the night into overdrive
Seamless segues through Shakedown Street → Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance
Brent Mydland’s bright, twinkling keyboards especially on Big Railroad Blues and The Wheel
Intense drum/space wall from Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann driving the second‑set momentum

band_performance.Jerry: Incendiary leads and scorching solos dominate, with hot tone on Let It Grow, double‑time C.C. Rider and intense intros that drive the set.
band_performance.Phil: Thunderous bass bombs (especially on Passenger) and a driving presence throughout; provides solid foundation and occasional melodic bursts.
band_performance.Bob: Enthusiastic vocal shouts on C.C. Rider and lively crowd interaction, adding humor and energy despite a minor vocal slip on Sugar Magnolia.
band_performance.Brent: Bright, ‘twinkle‑tinkle’ keyboard textures that complement the high‑octane playing; standout work on Shakedown Street, Big Railroad Blues and The Wheel.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful, thundering drumming in the drum/space sections, creating a scary intensity that fuels the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, dynamic drumming that locks in with Mickey, delivering a solid rhythmic backbone throughout the night.
